problem.title: Ugly Number
problem.description: An ugly number is a positive integer whose prime factors are limited to 2, 3, and 5. Given an integer n, return true if n is an ugly number.
assumptions: Ugly numbers have only prime factors 2, 3, 5.

example.explanation: Ugly numbers: 1,2,3,4,5,6,8,9,10,12. 10th is 12. Use three pointers for multiples of 2,3,5.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Prime Factorization
solutions.description: Repeatedly divide n by 2, 3, and 5 until none of them divides it evenly; if the result is 1 then all prime factors were among {2, 3, 5}. This works because every integer has a unique prime factorization, so exhausting these three primes leaves 1 if and only if no other prime factor exists. The key insight is that order of division doesn't matter — any combination of the three primes can be removed in any sequence.
